sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Intersect's Parameter Committee proposes to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the van Rossem hard fork (to Protocol Version 11), to enable all Plutus..., Note that the new primitives will not be enabled in Protocol version 10, but only following the enactment of the hard fork to Protocol Version 11., However, the changes for existing primitives will happen immediately on enactment of this governance action., This governance action is motivated by three objectives: First, following the hard fork to Protocol Version 11, new Plutus primitives become available.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect's Parameter Committee proposes to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the van Rossem hard fork (to Protocol Version 11), to enable all Plutus...

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Note that the new primitives will not be enabled in Protocol version 10, but only following the enactment of the hard fork to Protocol Version 11.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): However, the changes for existing primitives will happen immediately on enactment of this governance action.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): This governance action is motivated by three objectives: First, following the hard fork to Protocol Version 11, new Plutus primitives become available.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): This proposal provides the necessary cost model settings so that these primitives can be used following the van Rossem hard fork.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): Second, the action also ensures that Plutus primitives previously limited to Plutus V3 are available in Plutus V1 and Plutus V2.
missing: Independent evidence for: Intersect's Parameter Committee proposes to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the van Rossem hard fork (to Protocol Version 11), to enable all Plutus...
Independent evidence for: Note that the new primitives will not be enabled in Protocol version 10, but only following the enactment of the hard fork to Protocol Version 11.
Independent evidence for: However, the changes for existing primitives will happen immediately on enactment of this governance action.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "This governance action is motivated by three objectives: First, following the hard fork to Protocol Version 11, new Plutus primitives become available." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Intersect's Parameter Committee proposes to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the van Rossem hard fork (to Protocol Version 11), to enable all Plutus..." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
